pr.title: Group the wiki TU list into progress sections
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Updated the wiki translation-unit generator to split the generated TU list into progress sections, expose separate .text and data percentages, preserve assignee round-tripping for active tables, and clean up wiki formatting details requested in review.

key_files.role: Changed the wiki TU page writer: buckets units into progress sections, formats rows and percentages, normalizes assignee display, and adjusts generated Markdown headers/counts.
evidence_refs: pr_body: groups generated page into three sections and separates .text % from data %

what_changed.text: Replaced one flat Translation Units table with three generated sections: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added in_progress, data_left, linked buckets and three section print blocks

what_changed.text: Classified units by `metadata.complete` first, then by whether `matched_code_percent` is at least 100, otherwise treating them as in progress.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: bucket loop checks metadata.complete before matched_code_percent >= 100

what_changed.text: Added separate `.text %` and `data %` cells via `percent_cell`/`percent_cells`, with an em dash for sections whose total is zero.
evidence_refs: pr_body: active rows gain separate .text % and data % columns

what_changed.text: Removed the combined `%` plus checkmark completion column; completion is represented by section membership.
evidence_refs: pr_body: combined % + checkmark column is removed
raw_diff: removed linked = heavy_check_mark/x output and old table header

what_changed.text: Kept assignee columns at the same indices in active full tables so `read_wiki` can round-trip existing assignee entries.
evidence_refs: pr_body: assignee columns stay at same indices
raw_diff: full_header keeps Assignee Discord and GitHub after data %

what_changed.text: Adjusted generated Markdown formatting: counts are printed below headers to keep header anchors stable, size strings use ` `, and Discord handles are normalized to exactly one leading `@`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: count below header,   sizes, Discord handles
raw_diff: friendly_size replace spaces with   and assignee_cells lstrip('@')

smart_moves.text: Used `metadata.complete` as the canonical fully-linked signal rather than inferring completion only from code percentage.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Fully linked criterion is metadata.complete
raw_diff comment: complete is the canonical done flag

smart_moves.text: Separated code progress from data/linking progress so 100% `.text` units with remaining data work are visible instead of appearing finished.
evidence_refs: pr_body: TU that's 100% code but a few percent data is no longer indistinguishable from a finished one
raw_diff: data_left bucket and data % column

smart_moves.text: Placed section counts under headers rather than in header text, preserving stable wiki anchor URLs while still showing counts.
evidence_refs: pr_body: count printed on line below header so anchors stay stable

smart_moves.text: Factored row formatting into helper functions (`file_of`, `assignee_cells`, `percent_cells`, `row_full`, `row_slim`) while making the table structure differences explicit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added helper functions in write(args)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Progress dashboards should distinguish matched `.text` from data/linking completion; a 100% code percentage is not the same state as a fully linked TU.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `.text matched - data / linking left` section

naming_conventions.text: Generated wiki sections use explicit progress names: `.text in progress`, `.text matched - data / linking left`, and `Fully linked`.
Discord assignee display is normalized to exactly one leading `@` in the generated wiki table.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Discord handles normalized to exactly one leading @

review_feedback.feedback: Remove emoji from headers because they do not render in the table of contents.
Place counts below headers so section hyperlink URLs remain consistent.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: count print statements below each ## header

review_feedback.feedback: Do not use a `details` tag for these tables; reviewer believed the page did not need collapsible sections.
After running size strings through `humanfriendly`, replace spaces with ` `.
Discord usernames should start with exactly one `@`, whether the parsed value had one or not.
A later review approved the PR after the author stated the requested changes were made.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment by itsgrimetime: made all those changes

follow_up_queries: read_wiki assignee columns Discord GitHub wiki_tu
Translation Units wiki .text matched data linking left

evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: PR body plus author follow-up comment
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for tools/wiki_tu.py
notes: Evidence is strong for tooling behavior and review feedback because the PR body, review text, and diff all align.
No build, test, or live wiki validation output is included beyond the author's comment that the changes were live; no validation result is promoted.
This PR is tooling/documentation oriented and does not provide assembly matching tactics or source-code decomp evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Separate code progress from data/linking completion in TU progress views
accepted_candidate_records.text: For translation-unit progress reporting, treat matched `.text` and fully linked completion as separate states. Use `metadata.complete` for the fully linked bucket and a separate 100% `.text` but not complete bucket for data/linking-left work.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep generated wiki anchors stable by not embedding volatile counts in headers
accepted_candidate_records.text: When generated wiki section counts change often, print counts below the header instead of including them in header text so section anchor URLs remain stable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: count printed after each section header

source_update_candidates.title: tools/wiki_tu.py writes the Translation Units wiki progress page
source_update_candidates.text: `tools/wiki_tu.py write` generates the Translation Units wiki page, reads existing assignees with `read_wiki`, and emits TU progress sections based on `.text` progress, data progress, and `metadata.complete`.
evidence_refs: pr_body: `wiki_tu.py write` emitted every translation unit
raw_diff: write(args) reads wiki_path, read_wiki(md), and prints Translation Units sections
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-level tooling fact, but should be curated as source/path metadata rather than promoted by PR intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Progress reporting should not conflate text match with full linkage
source_update_candidates.text: For project progress displays, prefer separate signals for `.text` match, data match, and fully linked completion rather than a single combined percent/checkmark.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential global tooling/reporting convention supported by this PR, but it is not an existing loaded decomp standard and should be reviewed before adoption.

rejection_notes: Do not infer any decompilation match, objdiff result, or regression validation from this PR; the slice contains no build or objdiff evidence.
Do not generalize the reviewer comments into all generated Markdown policy without curator review; they were made in the context of this wiki TU page.
Do not treat the author's live wiki link as proof of long-term deployed behavior beyond the PR slice.
